2606.27797.title: Optimizing Teacher-Student Partitioning for Scalable Knowledge Distillation on HPC Systems
2606.27797.paper_summary: Knowledge Distillation (KD) enables training smaller student models under the guidance of larger teacher models, and the widely adopted TRL library implements it. Yet, TRL treats both models symmetrically, missing opportunities to exploit their pronounced asymmetry in memory footprint, and communication requirements. This paper presents an HPC-aware methodology for KD that decouples teacher and student partitioning efficiently. Our approach achieves up to 67% higher samples-per-second than TRL by avoiding unnecessary teacher-model data structures and selecting the best split strategy. We combine vertical and horizontal partitioning of models, deriving an analytical expression that identifies the existence of inflection points between splitting regimes. These results showed that exploiting teacher--student asymmetry through topology-aware parallelism notably accelerated GKD training on production HPC clusters at our company
2606.27797.paper_summary_zh: 知識蒸餾（KD）使得在較大的教師模型指導下訓練較小的學生模型成為可能，而廣泛採用的TRL庫實現了這一點。然而，TRL對兩個模型採取對稱處理，錯過了利用它們在記憶體佔用和通信需求方面明顯不對稱的機會。本文提出了一種考慮高效能計算（HPC）的KD方法論，能夠有效地解耦教師和學生的分區。我們的方法通過避免不必要的教師模型數據結構和選擇最佳的拆分策略，實現了比TRL高出67%的樣本每秒數。我們結合了模型的垂直和水平分區，推導出一個分析表達式，識別出拆分模式之間的拐點存在。這些結果表明，通過拓撲感知的並行性來利用教師與學生的不對稱性，顯著加速了我們公司在生產HPC集群上的GKD訓練。
2606.27797.author: Adrian P. Dieguez et.al.
2606.27797.authors: Adrian P. Dieguez, Victor Conchello Vendrell, Alex Batlle, Vinnam Kim, Jordi Ros-Giralt, Harris Teague
